title: Al Baraka Bank Egypt (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Al Baraka Bank Egypt in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Al Baraka Bank Egypt?
faq.answer: Al Baraka Bank Egypt is a leading Islamic bank operating in Egypt, providing Sharia-compliant banking services for nearly 40 years. The bank is a subsidiary of the Al Baraka Banking Group, a Bahrain-based international Islamic financial institution with operations across 13 countries and more than 600 branches globally. As one of the pioneers of Islamic banking in Egypt, Al Baraka Bank Egypt offers retail, corporate, treasury, and investment banking services strictly in accordance with Islamic Sharia principles. The bank has established itself as a significant player in the Egyptian banking market, with recognition including being named the Fastest Growing Islamic Bank in 2022 by Global Business Outlook Magazine. It has been listed in Forbes' Egypt's Top 50 Companies consecutively in 2022, 2023, and 2024.

faq.question: When was Al Baraka Bank Egypt founded?
faq.answer: Al Baraka Bank Egypt was founded in 1980 as a joint venture between Al Baraka Banking Group and local investors. The bank was originally established under different names, including Egyptian Saudi Finance Bank and Al Ahram Bank, before adopting its current identity as Al Baraka Bank Egypt. Since its establishment, the bank has grown steadily over four decades, evolving from a small Islamic finance institution into one of Egypt's prominent Islamic banks. The bank's duration was initially set for 25 years, ending on April 28, 2030, though it continues to operate and expand its presence in the Egyptian market.

faq.question: Where is Al Baraka Bank Egypt headquartered?
faq.answer: Al Baraka Bank Egypt is headquartered in the Fifth Settlement area of Cairo, Egypt. The bank's official address is P.O. Box 84, Fifth Settlement, Arab Republic of Egypt, with postal code 11835. The headquarters serves as the central administrative hub for the bank's operations throughout Egypt. The Fifth Settlement location positions the bank in one of Cairo's modern commercial districts.

faq.question: Where does Al Baraka Bank Egypt operate?
faq.answer: Al Baraka Bank Egypt operates throughout Egypt with a network of 43 branches (as of 2025) covering major regions including Cairo, Alexandria, the Delta region, and Upper Egypt. The bank has branches strategically located across key Egyptian governorates to serve diverse customer segments. The bank has been implementing a comprehensive expansion plan, with recent openings in El-Mokattam, Beheira, and Beni Suef. Al Baraka Bank Egypt aims to increase its geographical reach by opening additional branches in uncovered and economically vibrant governorates, targeting a total of 9 new branches and 2 digital branches by the end of 2025 to cover most governorates of the Republic. Additionally, the bank operates a network of 163 ATMs strategically located across the country, with plans to add 100 more ATMs in 2025.

faq.question: What services does Al Baraka Bank Egypt offer?
faq.answer: Al Baraka Bank Egypt offers a comprehensive range of Sharia-compliant banking services across multiple segments: Personal Banking: Deposit accounts based on Mudaraba and Wakala principles, financing solutions, debit and credit cards (including Titanium, Platinum, and World cards), and savings certificates. Corporate Banking: Working capital finance, term finance, project finance, cash management services (collections, payments, liquidity management), trade finance solutions (letters of credit, guarantees, export finance), and syndicated loan participation for large-scale projects. Treasury Services: Sharia-compliant investment products including Sukuk, Murabaha, and Musharaka arrangements. Digital Banking Services: The bank launched a comprehensive mobile banking application in 2024 for both retail and corporate customers, featuring self-registration, biometric authentication (facial recognition and fingerprint), account management, instant transfers, and bill payments. The bank also offers updated internet banking services and is preparing to launch Chatbot and WhatsApp for Business services. The bank aims to achieve 50% digitally active customers by 2027. All banking operations and transactions are reviewed by an independent Sharia Supervisory Board to ensure compliance with Islamic principles.

faq.question: What makes Al Baraka Bank Egypt unique?
faq.answer: Al Baraka Bank Egypt distinguishes itself through several key features: Islamic Banking Pioneer: As one of Egypt's earliest Islamic banks with nearly 40 years of experience, the bank has deep expertise in Sharia-compliant financial solutions and maintains strict adherence to Islamic principles through an independent Sharia Supervisory Board. Digital Innovation Leadership: Al Baraka Bank Egypt is among the first four banks in Egypt to launch a corporate mobile banking application, demonstrating its commitment to digital transformation. The bank has won awards for "Fastest Growing Digital Transformation Bank – Egypt 2024" and targets achieving 50% digitally active customers by 2027. International Network: As part of the Al Baraka Banking Group, customers benefit from the group's extensive global presence across 14 countries with over 600 branches, facilitating international banking and trade finance operations. Sustainable Financing Focus: The bank provides financing and credit facilities to companies aligned with sustainable development objectives, including renewable energy, educational technology, and infrastructure projects, with approximately EGP 500 million in company investments by the end of 2024. Awards and Recognition: The bank has received multiple international awards, including "Best Local Currency Finance," "Best Restructuring in Africa" from EMEA Finance Magazine, and "Fastest Bank to Achieve the World Card Targets in Egypt" from MasterCard.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Al Baraka Bank Egypt?
faq.answer: Al Baraka Bank Egypt is an Islamic Commercial Bank operating under Egyptian banking law and Central Bank of Egypt regulations. The bank is licensed to practice all banking, financial, and commercial services that commercial banks are authorized to conduct, in compliance with Law No. 43 of 1974 (amended), Law No. 163 of 1957 (amended), and Law No. 120 of 1975. As an Islamic bank, it is specifically conditioned that all of the bank's activities must be non-usurious and carried out in strict compliance with the principles of Islamic Sharia. This classification makes it a universal Islamic bank, providing retail, corporate, investment, and treasury services while adhering to Islamic financial principles such as profit-and-loss sharing, prohibition of interest (riba), and ethical investment guidelines. The bank operates as a joint-stock company (S.A.E. - Société Anonyme Egyptienne) and is regulated by the Central Bank of Egypt. It also holds a license from the Financial Regulatory Authority (FRA) to underwrite and cover subscriptions in securitization transactions.
